verses.latin_vulgate: Quod cum audisset Chananæus rex Arad, qui habitabat ad meridiem, venisse scilicet Israël per exploratorum viam, pugnavit contra illum, et victor existens, duxit ex eo prædam.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיִּשְׁמַ֞ע הַכְּנַעֲנִ֤י מֶֽלֶךְ־עֲרָד֙ יֹשֵׁ֣ב הַנֶּ֔גֶב כִּ֚י בָּ֣א יִשְׂרָאֵ֔ל דֶּ֖רֶךְ הָאֲתָרִ֑ים וַיִּלָּ֙חֶם֙ בְּיִשְׂרָאֵ֔ל וַיִּ֥שְׁבְּ ׀ מִמֶּ֖נּוּ שֶֽׁבִי׃
verses.douay_rheims: And when king Arad the Chanaanite, who dwelt towards the south, had heard this, to wit, that Israel was come by the way of the spies, he fought against them, and overcoming them carried off their spoils.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja koska Arad Kanaanealaisten kuningas, joka asui etelän puolessa, kuuli Israelin tulleeksi vakoojain tien kautta, soti hän Israelia vastaan ja vei heistä monikahtoja vangiksi.
verses.king_james: And when king Arad the Canaanite, which dwelt in the south, heard tell that Israel came by the way of the spies; then he fought against Israel, and took some of them prisoners.

verses.latin_vulgate: At Israël voto se Domino obligans, ait : Si tradideris populum istum in manu mea, delebo urbes ejus.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיִּדַּ֨ר יִשְׂרָאֵ֥ל נֶ֛דֶר לַֽיהֹוָ֖ה וַיֹּאמַ֑ר אִם־נָתֹ֨ן תִּתֵּ֜ן אֶת־הָעָ֤ם הַזֶּה֙ בְּיָדִ֔י וְהַֽחֲרַמְתִּ֖י אֶת־עָרֵיהֶֽם׃
verses.douay_rheims: But Israel binding himself by vow to the Lord, said: If thou wilt deliver thus people into my hand, I will utterly destroy their cities.
verses.finnish_biblia: Niin lupasi Israel Herralle lupauksen ja sanoi: jos sinä annat tämän kansan minun käteni alle, niin minä kiroon heidän kaupunkinsa.
verses.king_james: And Israel vowed a vow unto the Lord, and said, If thou wilt indeed deliver this people into my hand, then I will utterly destroy their cities.

verses.latin_vulgate: Exaudivitque Dominus preces Israël, et tradidit Chananæum, quem ille interfecit subversis urbibus ejus : et vocavit nomen loci illius Horma, id est, anathema.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיִּשְׁמַ֨ע יְהֹוָ֜ה בְּק֣וֹל יִשְׂרָאֵ֗ל וַיִּתֵּן֙ אֶת־הַֽכְּנַעֲנִ֔י וַיַּחֲרֵ֥ם אֶתְהֶ֖ם וְאֶת־עָרֵיהֶ֑ם וַיִּקְרָ֥א שֵׁם־הַמָּק֖וֹם חׇרְמָֽה׃ {פ}
verses.douay_rheims: And the Lord heard the prayers of Israel, and delivered up the Chanaanite, and they cut them off and destroyed their cities: and they called the name of that place Horma, that is to say, Anathema.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja Herra kuuli Israelin rukouksen: ja antoi heille Kanaanealaiset, ja he kirosivat heitä ja heidän kaupungeitansa. Ja kutsui sen paikan Horma.
verses.king_james: And the Lord hearkened to the voice of Israel, and delivered up the Canaanites; and they utterly destroyed them and their cities: and he called the name of the place Hormah.

verses.latin_vulgate: Profecti sunt autem et de monte Hor, per viam quæ ducit ad mare Rubrum, ut circumirent terram Edom. Et tædere cœpit populum itineris ac laboris :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיִּסְע֞וּ מֵהֹ֤ר הָהָר֙ דֶּ֣רֶךְ יַם־ס֔וּף לִסְבֹ֖ב אֶת־אֶ֣רֶץ אֱד֑וֹם וַתִּקְצַ֥ר נֶֽפֶשׁ־הָעָ֖ם בַּדָּֽרֶךְ׃
verses.douay_rheims: And they marched from mount Hor, by the way that leadeth to the Red Sea, to compass the land of Edom. And the people began to be weary of their journey and labour:
verses.finnish_biblia: Sitte menivät he pois Horin vuoren tyköä, Punaisen meren tietä, vaeltamaan Edomilaisten maata ympäri. Ja kansa suuttui matkasta.
verses.king_james: And they journeyed from mount Hor by the way of the Red sea, to compass the land of Edom: and the soul of the people was much discouraged because of the way.

verses.latin_vulgate: locutusque contra Deum et Moysen, ait : Cur eduxisti nos de Ægypto, ut moreremur in solitudine ? deest panis, non sunt aquæ : anima nostra jam nauseat super cibo isto levissimo.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיְדַבֵּ֣ר הָעָ֗ם בֵּֽאלֹהִים֮ וּבְמֹשֶׁה֒ לָמָ֤ה הֶֽעֱלִיתֻ֙נוּ֙ מִמִּצְרַ֔יִם לָמ֖וּת בַּמִּדְבָּ֑ר כִּ֣י אֵ֥ין לֶ֙חֶם֙ וְאֵ֣ין מַ֔יִם וְנַפְשֵׁ֣נוּ קָ֔צָה בַּלֶּ֖חֶם הַקְּלֹקֵֽל׃
verses.douay_rheims: And speaking against God and Moses, they said: Why didst thou bring us out of Egypt, to die in the wilderness? There is no bread, nor have we any waters: our soul now loatheth this very light food.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja kansa puhui Jumalaa ja Mosesta vastaan: miksi sinä toit meitä Egyptistä korpeen kuolemaan? Sillä ei tässä ole leipää eikä vettä, ja meidän sielumme suuttuu tähän huonoon ruokaan.
verses.king_james: And the people spake against God, and against Moses, Wherefore have ye brought us up out of Egypt to die in the wilderness? for there is no bread, neither is there any water; and our soul loatheth this light bread.

verses.latin_vulgate: Quam ob rem misit Dominus in populum ignitos serpentes, ad quorum plagas et mortes plurimorum,
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיְשַׁלַּ֨ח יְהֹוָ֜ה בָּעָ֗ם אֵ֚ת הַנְּחָשִׁ֣ים הַשְּׂרָפִ֔ים וַֽיְנַשְּׁכ֖וּ אֶת־הָעָ֑ם וַיָּ֥מׇת עַם־רָ֖ב מִיִּשְׂרָאֵֽל׃
verses.douay_rheims: Wherefore the Lord sent among the people fiery serpents, which bit them and killed many of them.
verses.finnish_biblia: Niin Herra lähetti kansan sekaan tuliset kärmeet, jotka kansaa purivat, niin että paljo kansaa Israelista kuoli.
verses.king_james: And the Lord sent fiery serpents among the people, and they bit the people; and much people of Israel died.

verses.latin_vulgate: venerunt ad Moysen, atque dixerunt : Peccavimus, quia locuti sumus contra Dominum et te : ora ut tollat a nobis serpentes. Oravitque Moyses pro populo,
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיָּבֹא֩ הָעָ֨ם אֶל־מֹשֶׁ֜ה וַיֹּאמְר֣וּ חָטָ֗אנוּ כִּֽי־דִבַּ֤רְנוּ בַֽיהֹוָה֙ וָבָ֔ךְ הִתְפַּלֵּל֙ אֶל־יְהֹוָ֔ה וְיָסֵ֥ר מֵעָלֵ֖ינוּ אֶת־הַנָּחָ֑שׁ וַיִּתְפַּלֵּ֥ל מֹשֶׁ֖ה בְּעַ֥ד הָעָֽם׃
verses.douay_rheims: Upon which they came to Moses, and said; We have sinned, because we have spoken against the Lord and thee: pray that he may take away these serpents from us. And Moses prayed for the people.
verses.finnish_biblia: Niin tuli kansa Moseksen tykö, ja sanoivat: me teimme syntiä, puhuen Herraa ja sinua vastaan. Rukoile Herraa nämät kärmeet meidän seastamme ottamaan pois: ja Moses rukoili kansan edestä.
verses.king_james: Therefore the people came to Moses, and said, We have sinned, for we have spoken against the Lord, and against thee; pray unto the Lord, that he take away the serpents from us. And Moses prayed for the people.

verses.latin_vulgate: et locutus est Dominus ad eum : Fac serpentem æneum, et pone eum pro signo : qui percussus aspexerit eum, vivet.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֹּ֨אמֶר יְהֹוָ֜ה אֶל־מֹשֶׁ֗ה עֲשֵׂ֤ה לְךָ֙ שָׂרָ֔ף וְשִׂ֥ים אֹת֖וֹ עַל־נֵ֑ס וְהָיָה֙ כׇּל־הַנָּשׁ֔וּךְ וְרָאָ֥ה אֹת֖וֹ וָחָֽי׃
verses.douay_rheims: And the Lord said to him: Make a brazen serpent, and set it up for a sign: whosoever being struck shall look on it, shall live.
verses.finnish_biblia: Niin sanoi Herra Mosekselle: tee sinulles tulinen kärme, ja nosta se merkiksi ylös: ja tapahtuu, että se joka purtu on, kuin hän katsoo sen päälle, niin hän elää.
verses.king_james: And the Lord said unto Moses, Make thee a fiery serpent, and set it upon a pole: and it shall come to pass, that every one that is bitten, when he looketh upon it, shall live.

verses.latin_vulgate: Fecit ergo Moyses serpentem æneum, et posuit eum pro signo : quem cum percussi aspicerent, sanabantur.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיַּ֤עַשׂ מֹשֶׁה֙ נְחַ֣שׁ נְחֹ֔שֶׁת וַיְשִׂמֵ֖הוּ עַל־הַנֵּ֑ס וְהָיָ֗ה אִם־נָשַׁ֤ךְ הַנָּחָשׁ֙ אֶת־אִ֔ישׁ וְהִבִּ֛יט אֶל־נְחַ֥שׁ הַנְּחֹ֖שֶׁת וָחָֽי׃
verses.douay_rheims: Moses therefore made a brazen serpent, and set it up for a sign: which when they that were bitten looked upon, they were healed.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja Moses teki vaskikärmeen, ja nosti sen merkiksi ylös: ja tapahtui, että jos joku kärmeeltä purtiin, ja katsahti vaskikärmeen päälle, niin hän jäi elämään.
verses.king_james: And Moses made a serpent of brass, and put it upon a pole, and it came to pass, that if a serpent had bitten any man, when he beheld the serpent of brass, he lived.

verses.latin_vulgate: Profectique filii Israël castrametati sunt in Oboth.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיִּסְע֖וּ בְּנֵ֣י יִשְׂרָאֵ֑ל וַֽיַּחֲנ֖וּ בְּאֹבֹֽת׃
verses.douay_rheims: And the children of Israel setting forwards camped in Oboth.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja Israelin lapset läksivät, ja sioittivat heitänsä Obotiin.
verses.king_james: And the children of Israel set forward, and pitched in Oboth.

verses.latin_vulgate: Unde egressi fixere tentoria in Jeabarim, in solitudine quæ respicit Moab contra orientalem plagam.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיִּסְע֖וּ מֵאֹבֹ֑ת וַֽיַּחֲנ֞וּ בְּעִיֵּ֣י הָֽעֲבָרִ֗ים בַּמִּדְבָּר֙ אֲשֶׁר֙ עַל־פְּנֵ֣י מוֹאָ֔ב מִמִּזְרַ֖ח הַשָּֽׁמֶשׁ׃
verses.douay_rheims: And departing thence they pitched their tents in Jeabarim, in the wilderness, that faceth Moab toward the east.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja läksivät Obotista, ja sioittivat itsensä Abarimin mäen tykö, siihen korpeen, joka Moabin kohdalla on, auringon nousemiseen päin.
verses.king_james: And they journeyed from Oboth, and pitched at Ije–abarim, in the wilderness which is before Moab, toward the sunrising.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et inde moventes, venerunt ad torrentem Zared.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: מִשָּׁ֖ם נָסָ֑עוּ וַֽיַּחֲנ֖וּ בְּנַ֥חַל זָֽרֶד׃
verses.douay_rheims: And removing from thence, they came to the torrent Zared:
verses.finnish_biblia: Sieltä läksivät he, ja sioittivat heitänsä Saredin ojan tykö.
verses.king_james: From thence they removed, and pitched in the valley of Zared.

verses.latin_vulgate: Quem relinquentes castrametati sunt contra Arnon, quæ est in deserto, et prominet in finibus Amorrhæi. Siquidem Arnon terminus est Moab, dividens Moabitas et Amorrhæos.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: מִשָּׁם֮ נָסָ֒עוּ֒ וַֽיַּחֲנ֗וּ מֵעֵ֤בֶר אַרְנוֹן֙ אֲשֶׁ֣ר בַּמִּדְבָּ֔ר הַיֹּצֵ֖א מִגְּבֻ֣ל הָֽאֱמֹרִ֑י כִּ֤י אַרְנוֹן֙ גְּב֣וּל מוֹאָ֔ב בֵּ֥ין מוֹאָ֖ב וּבֵ֥ין הָאֱמֹרִֽי׃
verses.douay_rheims: Which they left and encamped over against Arnon, which is in the desert and standeth out on the borders of the Amorrhite. For Arnon is the border of Moab, dividing the Moabites and the Amorrhites.
verses.finnish_biblia: Niin he sieltä menivät ulos, ja sioittivat heitänsä tälle puolen Arnonia, joka korvessa on, ja tulee Amorilaisten maan ääristä; sillä Arnon on Moabin ja Amorin välillä.
verses.king_james: From thence they removed, and pitched on the other side of Arnon, which is in the wilderness that cometh out of the coasts of the Amorites: for Arnon is the border of Moab, between Moab and the Amorites.

verses.latin_vulgate: Unde dicitur in libro bellorum Domini :   Sicut fecit in mari Rubro,  sic faciet in torrentibus Arnon.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: עַל־כֵּן֙ יֵֽאָמַ֔ר בְּסֵ֖פֶר מִלְחֲמֹ֣ת יְהֹוָ֑ה אֶת־וָהֵ֣ב בְּסוּפָ֔ה וְאֶת־הַנְּחָלִ֖ים אַרְנֽוֹן׃
verses.douay_rheims: Wherefore it is said in the book of the wars of the Lord: As he did in the Red Sea, so will he do in the streams of Arnon.
verses.finnish_biblia: Siitä sanotaan Herran sotain kirjassa: Vaheb Suphassa, ja Arnonin ojat.
verses.king_james: Wherefore it is said in the book of the wars of the Lord, What he did in the Red sea, and in the brooks of Arnon,

verses.latin_vulgate: Scopuli torrentium inclinati sunt, ut requiescerent in Ar,  et recumberent in finibus Moabitarum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְאֶ֙שֶׁד֙ הַנְּחָלִ֔ים אֲשֶׁ֥ר נָטָ֖ה לְשֶׁ֣בֶת עָ֑ר וְנִשְׁעַ֖ן לִגְב֥וּל מוֹאָֽב׃
verses.douay_rheims: The rocks of the torrents were bowed down that they might rest in Ar, and lie down in the borders of the Moabites.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja sen ojan juoksu, joka poikkee Arin asumiseen, ja ulottuu Moabin rajaan.
verses.king_james: And at the stream of the brooks that goeth down to the dwelling of Ar, and lieth upon the border of Moab.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ex eo loco apparuit puteus, super quo locutus est Dominus ad Moysen : Congrega populum, et dabo ei aquam.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וּמִשָּׁ֖ם בְּאֵ֑רָה הִ֣וא הַבְּאֵ֗ר אֲשֶׁ֨ר אָמַ֤ר יְהֹוָה֙ לְמֹשֶׁ֔ה אֱסֹף֙ אֶת־הָעָ֔ם וְאֶתְּנָ֥ה לָהֶ֖ם מָֽיִם׃ {ס}
verses.douay_rheims: When they went from that place, the well appeared whereof the Lord said to Moses: Gather the people together, and I will give them water.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja sieltä läksivät he Beraan, se on se kaivo, josta Herra sanoi Mosekselle: kokoa kansa, ja minä annan heille vettä.
verses.king_james: And from thence they went to Beer: that is the well whereof the Lord spake unto Moses, Gather the people together, and I will give them water.

verses.latin_vulgate: Tunc cecinit Israël carmen istud :  Ascendat puteus. Concinebant :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אָ֚ז יָשִׁ֣יר יִשְׂרָאֵ֔ל אֶת־הַשִּׁירָ֖ה הַזֹּ֑את עֲלִ֥י בְאֵ֖ר עֱנוּ־לָֽהּ׃
verses.douay_rheims: Then Israel sung this song: Let the well spring up. They sung thereto:
verses.finnish_biblia: Silloin veisasi Israel tämän virren: Nouse, kaivo, veisatkaat hänestä toinen toisellenne.
verses.king_james: Then Israel sang this song, Spring up, O well; sing ye unto it:

verses.latin_vulgate: Puteus, quem foderunt principes  et paraverunt duces multitudinis  in datore legis, et in baculis suis. De solitudine, Matthana.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: בְּאֵ֞ר חֲפָר֣וּהָ שָׂרִ֗ים כָּר֙וּהָ֙ נְדִיבֵ֣י הָעָ֔ם בִּמְחֹקֵ֖ק בְּמִשְׁעֲנֹתָ֑ם וּמִמִּדְבָּ֖ר מַתָּנָֽה׃
verses.douay_rheims: The well, which the princes dug, and the chiefs of the people prepared by the direction of the lawgiver, and with their staves. And they marched from the wilderness to Mathana.
verses.finnish_biblia: Tämä on se kaivo, jonka päämiehet kaivoivat, kansan johdattajat kaivoivat, lain opettajan kautta sauvallansa. Tästä korvesta matkustivat he Mattanaan.
verses.king_james: The princes digged the well, the nobles of the people digged it, by the direction of the lawgiver, with their staves. And from the wilderness they went to Mattanah:

verses.latin_vulgate: De Matthana in Nahaliel : de Nahaliel in Bamoth.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וּמִמַּתָּנָ֖ה נַחֲלִיאֵ֑ל וּמִנַּחֲלִיאֵ֖ל בָּמֽוֹת׃
verses.douay_rheims: From Mathana unto Nahaliel: from Nahaliel unto Bamoth.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja Mattanasta niin Nahalieliin, Ja Nahalielistä niin Bamotiin,
verses.king_james: And from Mattanah to Nahaliel: and from Nahaliel to Bamoth:

verses.latin_vulgate: De Bamoth, vallis est in regione Moab, in vertice Phasga, quod respicit contra desertum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וּמִבָּמ֗וֹת הַגַּיְא֙ אֲשֶׁר֙ בִּשְׂדֵ֣ה מוֹאָ֔ב רֹ֖אשׁ הַפִּסְגָּ֑ה וְנִשְׁקָ֖פָה עַל־פְּנֵ֥י הַיְשִׁימֹֽן׃ {פ}
verses.douay_rheims: From Bamoth, is a valley in the country of Moab, to the top of Phasga, which looked towards the desert.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja Bamotista siihen laaksoon, joka on Moabin kedolla, Pisgan kukkulaa kohden, joka katsahtaa korpeen päin.
verses.king_james: And from Bamoth in the valley, that is in the country of Moab, to the top of Pisgah, which looketh toward Jeshimon.

verses.latin_vulgate: Misit autem Israël nuntios ad Sehon regem Amorrhæorum, dicens :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיִּשְׁלַ֤ח יִשְׂרָאֵל֙ מַלְאָכִ֔ים אֶל־סִיחֹ֥ן מֶֽלֶךְ־הָאֱמֹרִ֖י לֵאמֹֽר׃
verses.douay_rheims: And Israel sent messengers to Sehon king of the Amorrhites, saying:
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja Israel lähetti sanansaattajat Sihonin Amorilaisten kuninkaan tykö, ja käski sanoa hänelle:
verses.king_james: And Israel sent messengers unto Sihon king of the Amorites, saying,

verses.latin_vulgate: Obsecro ut transire mihi liceat per terram tuam : non declinabimus in agros et vineas ; non bibemus aquas ex puteis : via regia gradiemur, donec transeamus terminos tuos.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אֶעְבְּרָ֣ה בְאַרְצֶ֗ךָ לֹ֤א נִטֶּה֙ בְּשָׂדֶ֣ה וּבְכֶ֔רֶם לֹ֥א נִשְׁתֶּ֖ה מֵ֣י בְאֵ֑ר בְּדֶ֤רֶךְ הַמֶּ֙לֶךְ֙ נֵלֵ֔ךְ עַ֥ד אֲשֶֽׁר־נַעֲבֹ֖ר גְּבֻלֶֽךָ׃
verses.douay_rheims: I beseech thee that I may have leave to pass through thy land: we will not go aside into the fields or the vineyards, we will not drink waters of the wells, we will go the king's highway, till we be past thy borders.
verses.finnish_biblia: Salli minun vaeltaa maakuntas lävitse: emme poikkee peltoihin, emmekä viinamäkiin, emmekä juo vettä kaivosta; maantietä myöten me vaellamme, siihenasti kuin me tulemme sinun maas ääristä ulos.
verses.king_james: Let me pass through thy land: we will not turn into the fields, or into the vineyards; we will not drink of the waters of the well: but we will go along by the king’s high way, until we be past thy borders.

verses.latin_vulgate: Qui concedere noluit ut transiret Israël per fines suos : quin potius exercitu congregato, egressus est obviam in desertum, et venit in Jasa, pugnavitque contra eum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְלֹא־נָתַ֨ן סִיחֹ֣ן אֶת־יִשְׂרָאֵל֮ עֲבֹ֣ר בִּגְבֻלוֹ֒ וַיֶּאֱסֹ֨ף סִיחֹ֜ן אֶת־כׇּל־עַמּ֗וֹ וַיֵּצֵ֞א לִקְרַ֤את יִשְׂרָאֵל֙ הַמִּדְבָּ֔רָה וַיָּבֹ֖א יָ֑הְצָה וַיִּלָּ֖חֶם בְּיִשְׂרָאֵֽל׃
verses.douay_rheims: And he would not grant that Israel should pass by his borders: but rather gathering an army, went forth to meet them in the desert, and came to Jasa and fought against them.
verses.finnish_biblia: Mutta Sihon ei sallinut Israelin vaeltaa maakuntansa lävitse, vaan Sihon kokosi kaiken väkensä, ja meni Israelia vastaan korpeen, ja tuli Jaksaan, ja soti Israelia vastaan.
verses.king_james: And Sihon would not suffer Israel to pass through his border: but Sihon gathered all his people together, and went out against Israel into the wilderness: and he came to Jahaz, and fought against Israel.

verses.latin_vulgate: A quo percussus est in ore gladii, et possessa est terra ejus ab Arnon usque Jeboc, et filios Ammon : quia forti præsidio tenebantur termini Ammonitarum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיַּכֵּ֥הוּ יִשְׂרָאֵ֖ל לְפִי־חָ֑רֶב וַיִּירַ֨שׁ אֶת־אַרְצ֜וֹ מֵֽאַרְנֹ֗ן עַד־יַבֹּק֙ עַד־בְּנֵ֣י עַמּ֔וֹן כִּ֣י עַ֔ז גְּב֖וּל בְּנֵ֥י עַמּֽוֹן׃
verses.douay_rheims: And he was slain by them with the edge of the sword, and they possessed his land from the Arnon unto the Jeboc, and to the confines of the children of Ammon: for the borders of the Ammonites, were kept with a strong garrison.
verses.finnish_biblia: Mutta Israel löi hänen miekan terällä, ja omisti hänen maansa Arnonista Jabbokiin asti, ja vielä Ammonin lapsiin saakka; sillä Ammonin maan ääret olivat vahvat.
verses.king_james: And Israel smote him with the edge of the sword, and possessed his land from Arnon unto Jabbok, even unto the children of Ammon: for the border of the children of Ammon was strong.

verses.latin_vulgate: Tulit ergo Israël omnes civitates ejus, et habitavit in urbibus Amorrhæi, in Hesebon scilicet, et viculis ejus.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיִּקַּח֙ יִשְׂרָאֵ֔ל אֵ֥ת כׇּל־הֶעָרִ֖ים הָאֵ֑לֶּה וַיֵּ֤שֶׁב יִשְׂרָאֵל֙ בְּכׇל־עָרֵ֣י הָֽאֱמֹרִ֔י בְּחֶשְׁבּ֖וֹן וּבְכׇל־בְּנֹתֶֽיהָ׃
verses.douay_rheims: So Israel took all his cities, and dwelt in the cities of the Amorrhite, to wit, in Hesebon, and in the villages thereof.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja näin Israel sai kaikki nämät kaupungit, ja Israel asui kaikissa Amorilaisten kaupungeissa, Hesbonissa ja kaikissa sen tyttärissä.
verses.king_james: And Israel took all these cities: and Israel dwelt in all the cities of the Amorites, in Heshbon, and in all the villages thereof.

verses.latin_vulgate: Urbs Hesebon fuit Sehon regis Amorrhæi, qui pugnavit contra regem Moab : et tulit omnem terram, quæ ditionis illius fuerat usque Arnon.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כִּ֣י חֶשְׁבּ֔וֹן עִ֗יר סִיחֹ֛ן מֶ֥לֶךְ הָאֱמֹרִ֖י הִ֑וא וְה֣וּא נִלְחַ֗ם בְּמֶ֤לֶךְ מוֹאָב֙ הָֽרִאשׁ֔וֹן וַיִּקַּ֧ח אֶת־כׇּל־אַרְצ֛וֹ מִיָּד֖וֹ עַד־אַרְנֹֽן׃
verses.douay_rheims: Hesebon was the city of Sehon the king of the Amorrhites, who fought against the king of Moab: and took all the land, that had been of his dominion, as far as the Arnon.
verses.finnish_biblia: Sillä Hesbonin kaupunki oli Sihonin Amorilaisten kuninkaan oma, ja hän soti ennen Moabilaisten kuninkaan kanssa, ja otti häneltä kaiken hänen maansa Arnoniin asti.
verses.king_james: For Heshbon was the city of Sihon the king of the Amorites, who had fought against the former king of Moab, and taken all his land out of his hand, even unto Arnon.

verses.latin_vulgate: Idcirco dicitur in proverbio :   Venite in Hesebon : ædificetur, et construatur civitas Sehon :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: עַל־כֵּ֛ן יֹאמְר֥וּ הַמֹּשְׁלִ֖ים בֹּ֣אוּ חֶשְׁבּ֑וֹן תִּבָּנֶ֥ה וְתִכּוֹנֵ֖ן עִ֥יר סִיחֽוֹן׃
verses.douay_rheims: Therefore it is said in the proverb: Come into Hesebon, let the city of Sehon be built and set up:
verses.finnish_biblia: Siitä sananlaskussa sanotaan: tulkaat Hesboniin: rakennettakoon ja valmistettakoon Sihonin kaupunki;
verses.king_james: Wherefore they that speak in proverbs say, Come into Heshbon, let the city of Sihon be built and prepared:

verses.latin_vulgate: ignis egressus est de Hesebon, flamma de oppido Sehon,  et devoravit Ar Moabitarum, et habitatores excelsorum Arnon.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כִּי־אֵשׁ֙ יָֽצְאָ֣ה מֵֽחֶשְׁבּ֔וֹן לֶהָבָ֖ה מִקִּרְיַ֣ת סִיחֹ֑ן אָֽכְלָה֙ עָ֣ר מוֹאָ֔ב בַּעֲלֵ֖י בָּמ֥וֹת אַרְנֹֽן׃
verses.douay_rheims: A fire is gone out of Hesebon, a flame from the city of Sehon, and hath consumed Ar of the Moabites, and the inhabitants of the high places of the Arnon.
verses.finnish_biblia: Sillä tuli on käynyt Hesbonista ulos, ja liekki Sihonin kaupungista: se on kuluttanut Moabin Arin, ja Arnonin korkeuden asuvaiset.
verses.king_james: For there is a fire gone out of Heshbon, a flame from the city of Sihon: it hath consumed Ar of Moab, and the lords of the high places of Arnon.

verses.latin_vulgate: Væ tibi Moab ; peristi popule Chamos.  Dedit filios ejus in fugam, et filias in captivitatem regi Amorrhæorum Sehon.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אוֹי־לְךָ֣ מוֹאָ֔ב אָבַ֖דְתָּ עַם־כְּמ֑וֹשׁ נָתַ֨ן בָּנָ֤יו פְּלֵיטִם֙ וּבְנֹתָ֣יו בַּשְּׁבִ֔ית לְמֶ֥לֶךְ אֱמֹרִ֖י סִיחֽוֹן׃
verses.douay_rheims: Woe to thee Moab: thou art undone, O people of Chamos. He hath given his sons to flight, and his daughters into captivity to Sehon the king of the Amorrhites.
verses.finnish_biblia: Voi sinuas Moab! sinä Kamosin kansa olet hukkunut! Hän saatti poikansa pakolaiseksi ja hänen tyttärensä vangiksi, Sihonille Amorilaisten kuninkaalle.
verses.king_james: Woe to thee, Moab! thou art undone, O people of Chemosh: he hath given his sons that escaped, and his daughters, into captivity unto Sihon king of the Amorites.

verses.latin_vulgate: Jugum ipsorum disperiit ad Hesebon usque Dibon :  lassi pervenerunt in Nophe, et usque Medaba.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַנִּירָ֛ם אָבַ֥ד חֶשְׁבּ֖וֹן עַד־דִּיבֹ֑ן וַנַּשִּׁ֣ים עַד־נֹ֔פַח אֲשֶׁ֖רׄ עַד־מֵֽידְבָֽא׃
verses.douay_rheims: Their yoke is perished from Hesebon unto Dibon, they came weary to Nophe, and unto Medaba.
verses.finnish_biblia: Me olemme heitä ampuneet, Hesbon on kukistettu Diboniin asti: me hävitimme heitä hamaan Nophaan, joka Medebaan ulottuu.
verses.king_james: We have shot at them; Heshbon is perished even unto Dibon, and we have laid them waste even unto Nophah, which reacheth unto Medeba.

verses.latin_vulgate: Habitavit itaque Israël in terra Amorrhæi.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֵּ֙שֶׁב֙ יִשְׂרָאֵ֔ל בְּאֶ֖רֶץ הָאֱמֹרִֽי׃
verses.douay_rheims: So Israel dwelt in the land of the Amorrhite.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja niin Israel asui Amorilaisten maakunnassa.
verses.king_james: Thus Israel dwelt in the land of the Amorites.

verses.latin_vulgate: Misitque Moyses qui explorarent Jazer : cujus ceperunt viculos, et possederunt habitatores.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיִּשְׁלַ֤ח מֹשֶׁה֙ לְרַגֵּ֣ל אֶת־יַעְזֵ֔ר וַֽיִּלְכְּד֖וּ בְּנֹתֶ֑יהָ (ויירש) [וַיּ֖וֹרֶשׁ] אֶת־הָאֱמֹרִ֥י אֲשֶׁר־שָֽׁם׃
verses.douay_rheims: And Moses sent some to take a view of Jazer: and they took the villages of it, and conquered the inhabitants.
verses.finnish_biblia: Sitte lähetti Moses vakoomaan Jaeseria, ja he voittivat sen tyttärinensä, ja omistivat ne Amorilaiset, jotka siinä asuivat.
verses.king_james: And Moses sent to spy out Jaazer, and they took the villages thereof, and drove out the Amorites that were there.

verses.latin_vulgate: Verteruntque se, et ascenderunt per viam Basan, et occurrit eis Og, rex Basan, cum omni populo suo, pugnaturus in Edrai.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיִּפְנוּ֙ וַֽיַּעֲל֔וּ דֶּ֖רֶךְ הַבָּשָׁ֑ן וַיֵּצֵ֣א עוֹג֩ מֶֽלֶךְ־הַבָּשָׁ֨ן לִקְרָאתָ֜ם ה֧וּא וְכׇל־עַמּ֛וֹ לַמִּלְחָמָ֖ה אֶדְרֶֽעִי׃
verses.douay_rheims: And they turned themselves, and went up by the way of Basan, and Og the king of Basan came against them with all his people, to fight in Edrai.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja he palasivat ja menivät ylöspäin tietä Basaniin. Niin Og, Basanin kuningas, meni heitä vastaan, hän ja kaikki hänen väkensä, sotimaan Edreissä.
verses.king_james: And they turned and went up by the way of Bashan: and Og the king of Bashan went out against them, he, and all his people, to the battle at Edrei.

verses.latin_vulgate: Dixitque Dominus ad Moysen : Ne timeas eum, quia in manu tua tradidi illum, et omnem populum ac terram ejus : faciesque illi sicut fecisti Sehon, regi Amorrhæorum habitatori Hesebon.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֹּ֨אמֶר יְהֹוָ֤ה אֶל־מֹשֶׁה֙ אַל־תִּירָ֣א אֹת֔וֹ כִּ֣י בְיָדְךָ֞ נָתַ֧תִּי אֹת֛וֹ וְאֶת־כׇּל־עַמּ֖וֹ וְאֶת־אַרְצ֑וֹ וְעָשִׂ֣יתָ לּ֔וֹ כַּאֲשֶׁ֣ר עָשִׂ֗יתָ לְסִיחֹן֙ מֶ֣לֶךְ הָֽאֱמֹרִ֔י אֲשֶׁ֥ר יוֹשֵׁ֖ב בְּחֶשְׁבּֽוֹן׃
verses.douay_rheims: And the Lord said to Moses: Fear him not, for I have delivered him and all his people, and his country into thy hand: and thou shalt do to him as thou didst to Sehon the king of the Amorrhites, the inhabitant of Hesebon.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja Herra sanoi Mosekselle: älä pelkää häntä, sillä minä annoin hänen sinun käsiis, sekä kaiken hänen väkensä, että hänen maansa: ja sinun pitää tekemän hänelle niinkuin sinä Sihonille Amorilaisten kuninkaalle teit, joka Hesbonissa asui.
verses.king_james: And the Lord said unto Moses, Fear him not: for I have delivered him into thy hand, and all his people, and his land; and thou shalt do to him as thou didst unto Sihon king of the Amorites, which dwelt at Heshbon.

verses.latin_vulgate: Percusserunt igitur et hunc cum filiis suis, universumque populum ejus usque ad internecionem, et possederunt terram illius.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיַּכּ֨וּ אֹת֤וֹ וְאֶת־בָּנָיו֙ וְאֶת־כׇּל־עַמּ֔וֹ עַד־בִּלְתִּ֥י הִשְׁאִֽיר־ל֖וֹ שָׂרִ֑יד וַיִּֽירְשׁ֖וּ אֶת־אַרְצֽוֹ׃
verses.douay_rheims: So they slew him also with his sons, and all his people, not letting any one escape, and they possessed his land.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja he löivät hänen ja hänen poikansa ja hänen väkensä, siihenasti ettei yksikään elämään jäänyt, ja he valloittivat hänen maansa.
verses.king_james: So they smote him, and his sons, and all his people, until there was none left him alive: and they possessed his land.
